description: A rich, smooth soup traditionally made from lobster stock, aromatics, cream, and lobster meat. Bisque is known for its velvety texture and concentrated shellfish flavor rather than being a thin broth.
preparation_steps: Cook lobster shells with aromatics to build a flavorful stock.
Saute onion, celery, and carrot in butter, then add tomato paste and optional brandy or sherry.
Add the stock and simmer to develop flavor.
Blend and strain if a smoother texture is desired.
Stir in cream and lobster meat, season, and serve hot.

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein and minerals from shellfish and can be satisfying in a small portion.
health_advice.risks: Often high in saturated fat and sodium because of cream, butter, and stock reduction.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use more lobster stock and less cream if you want a lighter version while keeping flavor.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional lobster bisque is shellfish-based and usually contains dairy. Gluten-free suitability depends on whether flour is used as a thickener.

allergens: Possible gluten if flour is used
